criteria.notes: Uses @amsterdam/design-system-react components (PageHeader, PageFooter, Grid, Column, Spotlight, Breadcrumb, etc.) clearly rendered in the screenshot.
Amsterdam Design System tokens and CSS imported; screenshot shows correct Amsterdam Sans font, blue brand colors, and proper spacing.
Screenshot shows a clear main-content/sidebar grid layout (~8/4 column split) using ADS Grid and Column components.
PageHeader with Gemeente Amsterdam logo and nav links visible at top; PageFooter with Contact, Bezoekadres, and Volg Zuidas sections plus privacy links at bottom.
Newsletter form uses ADS-styled label, text input, and blue button consistent with Amsterdam Design System form components.
No banned libraries (MUI, shadcn, Chakra) present; only ADS, React, React Router, and Vite in dependencies.
All visible text—headings, paragraphs, navigation, footer, form labels—is entirely in Dutch.
Text uses clear, accessible Dutch with short sentences, common vocabulary, and consistent 'u' form consistent with B1/Heldere Taal.
Homepage shows exactly 5 substantive paragraphs with distinct headings; transcript confirms same structure for all 3 pages.
Content references real projects (Zuidasdok, A10 Zuid tunnel, Ravel en Valley, Brittenpassage), specific figures (9,000 homes, 40% social housing, 4 miljard euro).
Consistent professional/governmental tone throughout, using formal 'u' form and neutral informative style.
Three routed pages visible via react-router-dom: / (Overzicht), /infrastructuur, /wonen-werken, with nav links in header.
Newsletter signup form clearly visible at page bottom with 'Blijf op de hoogte' heading, email input, and 'Aanmelden' button.
Header navigation with Overzicht/Infrastructuur/Wonen en werken links plus sidebar with StandaloneLink navigation to other pages.
